root_cause: checkout-worker failed to pick up the rotated stripe_api_key from vault. Secret was rotated at 02:58:04 UTC; payment-gateway reloaded to v7 immediately, but checkout-worker caches secrets in-process for the pod lifetime and only reloads on SIGHUP or restart. It kept signing outbound calls with stripe_api_key v6, which payment-gateway now rejects as 401 invalid_api_key_version. Retries amplified the error rate and backed up the checkout queue in redis, which is what paged oncall as user-facing 5xx.

distractors.why_not_causal: 340ms blip on redis-replica-02, recovered, unrelated to primary queue writes

distractors.event: checkout-worker db pool saturation warn at 02:57:22
distractors.why_not_causal: pool 48/50, recovered without errors; pre-dates the 401s; submit path does not block on this pool
flag toggle on checkout.new_fraud_check is a plausible-looking deploy event but does not touch auth
different caller, different upstream (stripe-api), recovered on attempt 2
different route, different client, not in the checkout submit path

remediation.immediate: rolling restart of checkout-worker deployment to force re-read of stripe_api_key from vault
remediation.permanent: add SIGHUP handler to checkout-worker that reloads secrets without restart; subscribe to vault rotation webhook so secret version bumps trigger an in-process refresh within 30s
